contentBlocks.original: अथ नारायन हृदय स्तोत्रम्
contentBlocks.transliteration: atha nārāyana hṛdaya stōtram
contentBlocks.translation: Thus begins the Sri Narayana Hridaya Stotram.

contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्रीनारायणहृदयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य भार्गव ऋषिः, अनुष्टुप्छन्दः, श्रीलक्ष्मीनारायणो देवता, ॐ बीजं, नमश्शक्तिः, नारायणायेति कीलकं, श्रीलक्ष्मीनारायण प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrīnārāyaṇahṛdayastōtramantrasya bhārgava ṛṣiḥ, anuṣṭupChandaḥ, śrīlakṣmīnārāyaṇō dēvatā, ōṃ bījaṃ, namaśśaktiḥ, nārāyaṇāyēti kīlakaṃ, śrīlakṣmīnārāyaṇa prītyarthē japē viniyōgaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Dedication (Viniyoga): Of this Sri Narayana Hridaya Stotra mantra, the sage is Bhargava (Shukracharya), the metre is Anushtup, the deity is Sri Lakshmi Narayana, the seed syllable is Om, the power (shakti) is Namah, the pin (kilaka) is Narayanaya, and it is being recited for the gratification of Sri Lakshmi Narayana.

contentBlocks.original:
करन्यासः ।
ॐ नारायणः परं ज्योतिरिति अङ्गुष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
नारायणः परं ब्रह्मेति तर्जनीभ्यां नमः ।
नारायणः परो देव इति मध्यमाभ्यां नमः ।
नारायणः परं धामेति अनामिकाभ्यां नमः ।
नारायणः परो धर्म इति कनिष्ठिकाभ्यां नमः ।
विश्वं नारायण इति करतलकरपृष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
अङ्गन्यासः ।
नारायणः परं ज्योतिरिति हृदयाय नमः ।
नारायणः परं ब्रह्मेति शिरसे स्वाहा ।
नारायणः परो देव इति शिखायै वौषट् ।
नारायणः परं धामेति कवचाय हुम् ।
नारायणः परो धर्म इति नेत्राभ्यां वौषट् ।
विश्वं नारायण इति अस्त्राय फट् ।
दिग्बन्धः ।
ॐ ऐन्द्र्यादिदशदिशं ॐ नमः सुदर्शनाय सहस्राराय हुं फट् बध्नामि नमश्चक्राय स्वाहा । इति प्रतिदिशं योज्यम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karanyāsaḥ
ōṃ nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ jyōtiriti aṅguṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ brahmēti tarjanībhyāṃ namaḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ parō dēva iti madhyamābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ dhāmēti anāmikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ parō dharma iti kaniṣṭhikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
viśvaṃ nārāyaṇa iti karatalakarapṛṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
aṅganyāsaḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ jyōtiriti hṛdayāya namaḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ brahmēti śirasē svāhā ।
nārāyaṇaḥ parō dēva iti śikhāyai vauṣaṭ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ dhāmēti kavachāya hum ।
nārāyaṇaḥ parō dharma iti nētrābhyāṃ vauṣaṭ ।
viśvaṃ nārāyaṇa iti astrāya phaṭ ।
digbandhaḥ ।
ōṃ aindryādidaśadiśaṃ ōṃ namaḥ sudarśanāya sahasrārāya huṃ phaṭ badhnāmi namaśchakrāya svāhā । iti pratidiśaṃ yōjyam ।
contentBlocks.translation: Kara Nyasa (hand-placement of mantras): Om — Narayana is the supreme light — salutations with the thumbs. Om — Narayana is the supreme Brahman — salutations with the forefingers. Om — Narayana is the supreme God — salutations with the middle fingers. Om — Narayana is the supreme abode — salutations with the ring fingers. Om — Narayana is the supreme Dharma — salutations with the little fingers. Om — The universe is Narayana — salutations with the backs and palms of the hands.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ ध्यानम्
उद्यादादित्यसङ्काशं पीतवासं चतुर्भुजम् ।
शङ्खचक्रगदापाणिं ध्यायेल्लक्ष्मीपतिं हरिम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha dhyānam
udyādādityasaṅkāśaṃ pītavāsaṃ chaturbhujam ।
śaṅkhachakragadāpāṇiṃ dhyāyēllakṣmīpatiṃ harim ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (meditation): Meditate on Hari, the lord of Lakshmi, who is radiant like the rising sun, dressed in yellow silk, four-armed, holding in his hands the conch, discus and mace.

contentBlocks.original:
त्रैलोक्याधारचक्रं तदुपरि कमठं तत्र चानन्तभोगी
तन्मध्ये भूमिपद्माङ्कुशशिखरदलं कर्णिकाभूतमेरुम् ।
तत्रस्थं शान्तमूर्तिं मणिमयमकुटं कुण्डलोद्भासिताङ्गं
लक्ष्मीनारायणाख्यं सरसिजनयनं सन्ततं चिन्तयामि ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trailōkyādhārachakraṃ tadupari kamaṭhaṃ tatra chānantabhōgī
tanmadhyē bhūmipadmāṅkuśaśikharadaḻaṃ karṇikābhūtamērum ।
tatrasthaṃ śāntamūrtiṃ maṇimayamakuṭaṃ kuṇḍalōdbhāsitāṅgaṃ
lakṣmīnārāyaṇākhyaṃ sarasijanayanaṃ santataṃ chintayāmi ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (continued): I ceaselessly contemplate Sri Lakshmi Narayana — the lotus-eyed lord who rests upon the cosmic serpent Ananta on the world-sustaining chakra, with Mount Meru as the pericarp of the lotus, adorned with a jewelled crown and gleaming earrings, of serene and peaceful form.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ मूलाष्टकम् ।
ओम् ॥ नारायणः परं ज्योतिरात्मा नारायणः परः ।
नारायणः परं ब्रह्म नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha mūlāṣṭakam ।
ōm ॥ nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ jyōtirātmā nārāyaṇaḥ paraḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ brahma nārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus begins the Mula Ashtakam. Om — Narayana is the supreme light and the Self. Narayana is supreme. Narayana is the supreme Brahman. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणः परो देवो धाता नारायणः परः ।
नारायणः परो धाता नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇaḥ parō dēvō dhātā nārāyaṇaḥ paraḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ parō dhātā nārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Narayana is the supreme God; Brahma (the creator) is Narayana, supreme. Narayana is the supreme creator. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणः परं धाम ध्यानं नारायणः परः ।
नारायण परो धर्मो नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇaḥ paraṃ dhāma dhyānaṃ nārāyaṇaḥ paraḥ ।
nārāyaṇa parō dharmō nārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Narayana is the supreme abode; meditation is Narayana, supreme. Narayana is the supreme Dharma. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणः परोवेद्यः विद्या नारायणः परः ।
विश्वं नारायणः साक्षान्नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇaḥ parōvēdyaḥ vidyā nārāyaṇaḥ paraḥ ।
viśvaṃ nārāyaṇaḥ sākṣānnārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Narayana is the supreme to be known; knowledge is Narayana, supreme. The entire universe is Narayana directly. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणाद्विधिर्जातो जातो नारायणाद्भवः ।
जातो नारायणादिन्द्रो नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇādvidhirjātō jātō nārāyaṇādbhavaḥ ।
jātō nārāyaṇādindrō nārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Brahma (the four-faced one) was born from Narayana; Shiva (Bhava) was born from Narayana; Indra was born from Narayana. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
रविर्नारायणस्तेजः चन्द्रो नारायणो महः ।
वह्निर्नारायणः साक्षान्नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ravirnārāyaṇastējaḥ chandrō nārāyaṇō mahaḥ ।
vahnirnārāyaṇaḥ sākṣānnārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The sun (Ravi) is the radiance of Narayana; the moon (Chandra) is the glory of Narayana; fire (Vahni) is directly Narayana. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायण उपास्यः स्याद्गुरुर्नारायणः परः ।
नारायणः परो बोधो नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇa upāsyaḥ syādgururnārāyaṇaḥ paraḥ ।
nārāyaṇaḥ parō bōdhō nārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Narayana is worthy of worship; the Guru is Narayana, supreme. Narayana is the supreme knowledge. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणः फलं मुख्यं सिद्धिर्नारायणः सुखम् ।
सेव्योनारायणः शुद्धो नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 8 ॥ [हरि]
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇaḥ phalaṃ mukhyaṃ siddhirnārāyaṇaḥ sukham ।
sēvyōnārāyaṇaḥ śuddhō nārāyaṇa namō'stu tē ॥ 8 ॥ [hari]
contentBlocks.translation: Narayana is the supreme fruit; liberation is Narayana, bliss. Narayana, the pure one, is to be served. Salutations to you, O Narayana. (8) [Hari — thus ends the Mula Ashtakam.]

contentBlocks.original:
अथ प्रार्थनादशकम् ।
नारायण त्वमेवासि दहराख्ये हृदि स्थितः ।
प्रेरकः प्रेर्यमाणानां त्वया प्रेरितमानसः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha prārthanādaśakam ।
nārāyaṇa tvamēvāsi daharākhyē hṛdi sthitaḥ ।
prērakaḥ prēryamāṇānāṃ tvayā prēritamānasaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus begins the Prarthana Dashakam (ten verses of prayer). O Narayana, you alone dwell in the lotus of the heart known as the cave (dahara). You are the inspirer; my mind is inspired by you. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदाज्ञां शिरसा धृत्वा जपामि जनपावनम् ।
नानोपासनमार्गाणां भवकृद्भावबोधकः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadājñāṃ śirasā dhṛtvā japāmi janapāvanam ।
nānōpāsanamārgāṇāṃ bhavakṛdbhāvabōdhakaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Holding your command upon my head, I recite this mantra that purifies all people. You are the one who gives meaning to all the paths of worship and who reveals their inner significance. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
भावार्थकृद्भवातीतो भव सौख्यप्रदो मम ।
त्वन्मायामोहितं विश्वं त्वयैव परिकल्पितम् ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhāvārthakṛdbhavātītō bhava saukhyapradō mama ।
tvanmāyāmōhitaṃ viśvaṃ tvayaiva parikalpitam ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You are the bestower of the bliss of worldly existence (bhava sukha) as well as the one beyond it. The entire universe, deluded by your maya, is fashioned by you alone. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदधिष्ठानमात्रेण सा वै सर्वार्थकारिणी ।
त्वमेतां च पुरस्कृत्य सर्वकामान्प्रदर्शय ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadadhiṣṭhānamātrēṇa sā vai sarvārthakāriṇī ।
tvamētāṃ cha puraskṛtya sarvakāmānpradarśaya ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By your mere presence, Lakshmi (your maya) accomplishes all purposes. Taking her as your companion, reveal to me the fulfilment of all desires. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
न मे त्वदन्यस्त्रातास्ति त्वदन्यन्न हि दैवतम् ।
त्वदन्यं न हि जानामि पालकं पुण्यवर्धनम् ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na mē tvadanyastrātāsti tvadanyanna hi daivatam ।
tvadanyaṃ na hi jānāmi pālakaṃ puṇyavardhanam ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: There is no saviour other than you for me; there is truly no deity other than you. I know no other protector, no other increaser of merit. (13)

contentBlocks.original:
यावत्सांसारिको भावो मनस्स्थो भावनात्मकः ।
तावत्सिद्धिर्भवेत्साध्या सर्वथा सर्वदा विभो ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāvatsāṃsārikō bhāvō manassthō bhāvanātmakaḥ ।
tāvatsiddhirbhavētsādhyā sarvathā sarvadā vibhō ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As long as worldly feeling remains in my mind, pervaded by the force of thought — may my spiritual attainment be accomplished in every way and always, O all-pervading Lord. (14)

contentBlocks.original:
पापिनामहमेवाग्र्यो दयालूनां त्वमग्रणीः ।
दयनीयो मदन्योऽस्ति तव कोऽत्र जगत्त्रये ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpināmahamēvāgryō dayāḻūnāṃ tvamagraṇīḥ ।
dayanīyō madanyō'sti tava kō'tra jagattrayē ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I am the foremost among sinners; you are the foremost among the compassionate. Who other than me deserves your compassion in all three worlds? (15)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वयाहं नैव सृष्टश्चेन्न स्यात्तव दयालुता ।
आमयो वा न सृष्टश्चेदौषधस्य वृथोदयः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvayāhaṃ naiva sṛṣṭaśchēnna syāttava dayāḻutā ।
āmayō vā na sṛṣṭaśchēdauṣadhasya vṛthōdayaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: If you had not created me, your quality of compassion would not exist. If disease had not been created, the arising of medicine would be purposeless. (16)

contentBlocks.original:
पापसङ्घपरिश्रान्तः पापात्मा पापरूपधृत् ।
त्वदन्यः कोऽत्र पापेभ्यस्त्रातास्ति जगतीतले ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpasaṅghapariśrāntaḥ pāpātmā pāparūpadhṛt ।
tvadanyaḥ kō'tra pāpēbhyastrātāsti jagatītalē ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Exhausted by the accumulation of sins, I am sinful in soul and sinful in form. Who other than you can rescue the sinful on this earth? (17)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वमेव माता च पिता त्वमेव
त्वमेव बन्धुश्च सखा त्वमेव ।
त्वमेव सेव्यश्च गुरुस्त्वमेव
त्वमेव सर्वं मम देव देव ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvamēva mātā cha pitā tvamēva
tvamēva bandhuścha sakhā tvamēva ।
tvamēva sēvyaścha gurustvamēva
tvamēva sarvaṃ mama dēva dēva ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You alone are my mother and father; you alone are my relative and friend. You alone are my master and guru. You alone are everything for me, O God of gods. (18)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रार्थनादशकं चैव मूलाष्टकमतः परम् ।
यः पठेच्छृणुयान्नित्यं तस्य लक्ष्मीः स्थिरा भवेत् ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prārthanādaśakaṃ chaiva mūlāṣṭakamataḥ param ।
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyānnityaṃ tasya lakṣmīḥ sthirā bhavēt ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever reads daily this Prarthana Dashakam (ten prayer verses) and the Mula Ashtakam that follows — for that person Lakshmi (prosperity) shall become stable. (19)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणस्य हृदयं सर्वाभीष्टफलप्रदम् ।
लक्ष्मीहृदयकं स्तोत्रं यदि चेत्तद्विनाकृतम् ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇasya hṛdayaṃ sarvābhīṣṭaphalapradam ।
lakṣmīhṛdayakaṃ stōtraṃ yadi chēttadvinākṛtam ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Hridayam of Narayana bestows all desired fruits. But the Lakshmi Hridayam stotra — if it is done without it — (20)

contentBlocks.original:
तत्सर्वं निष्फलं प्रोक्तं लक्ष्मीः क्रुद्ध्यति सर्वदा ।
एतत्सङ्कलितं स्तोत्रं सर्वकामफलप्रदम् ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tatsarvaṃ niṣphalaṃ prōktaṃ lakṣmīḥ kruddhyati sarvadā ।
ētatsaṅkalitaṃ stōtraṃ sarvakāmaphalapradam ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — that whole recitation is declared fruitless, and Lakshmi becomes displeased always. This combined stotra bestows the fruits of all desires. (21)

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीहृदयकं चैव तथा नारायणात्मकम् ।
जपेद्यः सङ्कलीकृत्य सर्वाभीष्टमवाप्नुयात् ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīhṛdayakaṃ chaiva tathā nārāyaṇātmakam ।
japēdyaḥ saṅkalīkṛtya sarvābhīṣṭamavāpnuyāt ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Lakshmi Hridayam and also the Narayana Hridayam — whoever recites them together in combination shall attain all desired objects. (22)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणस्य हृदयमादौ जप्त्वा ततः परम् ।
लक्ष्मीहृदयकं स्तोत्रं जपेन्नारायणं पुनः ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇasya hṛdayamādau japtvā tataḥ param ।
lakṣmīhṛdayakaṃ stōtraṃ japēnnārāyaṇaṃ punaḥ ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: First recite the Narayana Hridayam, then after that recite the Lakshmi Hridayam stotra, then Narayana again. (23)

contentBlocks.original:
पुनर्नारायणं जप्त्वा पुनर्लक्ष्मीनुतिं जपेत् ।
पुनर्नारायणं जाप्यं सङ्कलीकरणं भवेत् ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
punarnārāyaṇaṃ japtvā punarlakṣmīnutiṃ japēt ।
punarnārāyaṇaṃ jāpyaṃ saṅkalīkaraṇaṃ bhavēt ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having recited Narayana again, then recite the Lakshmi praise again. Then recite Narayana once more — this becomes the combined (sankalita) recitation. (24)

contentBlocks.original:
एवं मध्ये द्विवारेण जपेत्सङ्कलितं तु तत् ।
लक्ष्मीहृदयकं स्तोत्रं सर्वकामप्रकाशितम् ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēvaṃ madhyē dvivārēṇa japētsaṅkalitaṃ tu tat ।
lakṣmīhṛdayakaṃ stōtraṃ sarvakāmaprakāśitam ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus in the middle, by reciting twice, the combined Lakshmi Hridayam stotra, which illuminates all desires, is complete. (25)

contentBlocks.original:
तद्वज्जपादिकं कुर्यादेतत्सङ्कलितं शुभम् ।
सर्वान्कामानवाप्नोति आधिव्याधिभयं हरेत् ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tadvajjapādikaṃ kuryādētatsaṅkalitaṃ śubham ।
sarvānkāmānavāpnōti ādhivyādhibhayaṃ harēt ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Similarly, one should perform the japa and other practices of this combined auspicious stotra. One attains all desires and removes all mental and physical afflictions and fears. (26)

contentBlocks.original:
गोप्यमेतत्सदा कुर्यान्न सर्वत्र प्रकाशयेत् ।
इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रं प्राप्तं ब्रह्मादिकैः पुरा ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gōpyamētatsadā kuryānna sarvatra prakāśayēt ।
iti guhyatamaṃ śāstraṃ prāptaṃ brahmādikaiḥ purā ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This should always be kept secret; it should not be revealed everywhere. This most secret scripture was attained by Brahma and others in ancient times. (27)

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मात्सर्वप्रयत्नेन गोपयेत्साधयेसुधीः ।
यत्रैतत्पुस्तकं तिष्ठेल्लक्ष्मीनारायणात्मकम् ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmātsarvaprayatnēna gōpayētsādhayēsudhīḥ ।
yatraitatpustakaṃ tiṣṭhēllakṣmīnārāyaṇātmakam ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Therefore, with every effort, the wise person should guard it and practise it. Wherever this book of Lakshmi Narayana resides — (28)

contentBlocks.original:
भूतपैशाचवेताल भयं नैव तु सर्वदा ।
लक्ष्मीहृदयकं प्रोक्तं विधिना साधयेत्सुधीः ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūtapaiśāchavētāḻa bhayaṃ naiva tu sarvadā ।
lakṣmīhṛdayakaṃ prōktaṃ vidhinā sādhayētsudhīḥ ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — there shall be no fear from ghosts, evil spirits and vampires (vetala), ever. The Lakshmi Hridayam should be practised by the wise according to the prescribed method. (29)

contentBlocks.original:
भृगुवारे च रात्रौ च पूजयेत्पुस्तकद्वयम् ।
सर्वथा सर्वदा सत्यं गोपयेत्साधयेत्सुधीः ।
गोपनात्साधनाल्लोके धन्यो भवति तत्त्वतः ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhṛguvārē cha rātrau cha pūjayētpustakadvayam ।
sarvathā sarvadā satyaṃ gōpayētsādhayētsudhīḥ ।
gōpanātsādhanāllōkē dhanyō bhavati tattvataḥ ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: On Fridays (Bhriguvara) and at night, one should worship the two books (texts). The wise person who guards and practises this becomes truly blessed in the world. (30)

contentBlocks.original: इत्यथर्वरहस्ये उत्तरभागे नारायणहृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: ityatharvarahasyē uttarabhāgē nārāyaṇahṛdayaṃ sampūrṇam ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Narayana Hridayam from the Atharva Rahasya (secret portion of the Atharvaveda).

contentBlocks.original: अथ लक्ष्मी हृदय स्तोत्रम्
contentBlocks.transliteration: atha lakṣmī hṛdaya stōtram
contentBlocks.translation: Thus begins the Sri Lakshmi Hridaya Stotram.

contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्री महालक्ष्मीहृदयस्तोत्र महामन्त्रस्य भार्गव ऋषिः, अनुष्टुपादीनि नानाछन्दांसि, आद्यादि श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता, श्रीं बीजं, ह्रीं शक्तिः, ऐं कीलकं, आद्यादिमहालक्ष्मी प्रसादसिद्ध्यर्थं जपे विनियोगः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrī mahālakṣmīhṛdayastōtra mahāmantrasya bhārgava ṛṣiḥ, anuṣṭupādīni nānāChandāṃsi, ādyādi śrīmahālakṣmīrdēvatā, śrīṃ bījaṃ, hrīṃ śaktiḥ, aiṃ kīlakaṃ, ādyādimahālakṣmī prasādasiddhyarthaṃ japē viniyōgaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dedication (Viniyoga): Of this Sri Mahalakshmi Hridaya Stotra Mahamantra, the sage is Bhargava, the metres are Anushtup and others, the deity is the primal Mahalakshmi, the seed (bija) is Shrim, the power (shakti) is Hrim, the pin (kilaka) is Aim, and it is being recited for the gratification of the primal Mahalakshmi.

contentBlocks.original:
ऋष्यादिन्यासः
ॐ भार्गवृषये नमः शिरसि ।
ॐ अनुष्टुपादिनानाछन्दोभ्यो नमो मुखे ।
ॐ आद्यादिश्रीमहालक्ष्मी देवतायै नमो हृदये ।
ॐ श्रीं बीजाय नमो गुह्ये ।
ॐ ह्रीं शक्तये नमः पादयोः ।
ॐ ऐं कीलकाय नमो नाभौ ।
ॐ विनियोगाय नमः सर्वाङ्गे ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṛṣyādinyāsaḥ
ōṃ bhārgavṛṣayē namaḥ śirasi ।
ōṃ anuṣṭupādinānāChandōbhyō namō mukhē ।
ōṃ ādyādiśrīmahālakṣmī dēvatāyai namō hṛdayē ।
ōṃ śrīṃ bījāya namō guhyē ।
ōṃ hrīṃ śaktayē namaḥ pādayōḥ ।
ōṃ aiṃ kīlakāya namō nābhau ।
ōṃ viniyōgāya namaḥ sarvāṅgē ।
contentBlocks.translation: Rishi Nyasa (sage-placement): Om — salutations to the sage Bhargava on the head. Om — salutations to the various metres (Anushtup etc.) on the face. Om — salutations to the deity, the primal Mahalakshmi, on the heart. Om — salutations to the seed Shrim on the private parts. Om — salutations to the power Hrim on the feet. Om — salutations to the pin Aim on all limbs.

contentBlocks.original:
करन्यासः
ॐ श्रीं अङ्गुष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं तर्जनीभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ऐं मध्यमाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ श्रीं अनामिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं कनिष्ठिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ऐं करतल करपृष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karanyāsaḥ
ōṃ śrīṃ aṅguṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ tarjanībhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ aiṃ madhyamābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ anāmikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ kaniṣṭhikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ aiṃ karatala karapṛṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Kara Nyasa (hand-placement): Om Shrim — salutations with the thumbs. Om Hrim — salutations with the forefingers. Om Aim — salutations with the middle fingers. Om Shrim — salutations with the ring fingers. Om Hrim — salutations with the little fingers. Om Aim — salutations with the backs and palms of the hands.

contentBlocks.original:
अङ्गन्यासः
ॐ श्रीं हृदयाय नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं शिरसे स्वाहा ।
ॐ ऐं शिखायै वषट् ।
ॐ श्रीं कवचाय हुम् ।
ॐ ह्रीं नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट् ।
ॐ ऐं अस्त्राय फट् ।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं ऐं इति दिग्बन्धः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṅganyāsaḥ
ōṃ śrīṃ hṛdayāya namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ śirasē svāhā ।
ōṃ aiṃ śikhāyai vaṣaṭ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ kavachāya hum ।
ōṃ hrīṃ nētratrayāya vauṣaṭ ।
ōṃ aiṃ astrāya phaṭ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ aiṃ iti digbandhaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Anga Nyasa (body-placement): Om Shrim — salutations to the heart. Om Hrim — Svaha to the head. Om Aim — Vashat to the tuft of hair. Om Shrim — Hum to the armour. Om Hrim — Vaushat to the three eyes. Om Aim — Phat to the weapon. Om Shrim Hrim Aim — thus bind the ten directions.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ ध्यानम्
हस्तद्वयेन कमले धारयन्तीं स्वलीलया ।
हारनूपुरसंयुक्तां लक्ष्मीं देवीं विचिन्तये ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha dhyānam
hastadvayēna kamalē dhārayantīṃ svalīlayā ।
hāranūpurasaṃyuktāṃ lakṣmīṃ dēvīṃ vichintayē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (meditation): I contemplate Goddess Lakshmi who holds two lotuses playfully, adorned with necklaces and anklets.

contentBlocks.original:
कौशेयपीतवसनामरविन्दनेत्रां
पद्मद्वयाभयवरोद्यतपद्महस्ताम् ।
उद्यच्छतार्कसदृशीं परमाङ्कसंस्थां
ध्यायेद्विधीशनतपादयुगां जनित्रीम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kauśēyapītavasanāmaravindanētrāṃ
padmadvayābhayavarōdyatapadmahastām ।
udyachChatārkasadṛśīṃ paramāṅkasaṃsthāṃ
dhyāyēdvidhīśanatapādayugāṃ janitrīm ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (continued): I meditate on the Mother, dressed in silk of golden yellow, with lotus eyes, holding two lotus flowers and a golden vessel, her hands in gestures of protection and boon-giving, radiant as the risen hundred suns, seated in the lap of the cosmic being (Vishnu), adorned with creepers at her feet and glorified by Brahma and Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
पीतवस्त्रां सुवर्णाङ्गीं पद्महस्तद्वायान्विताम् ।
लक्ष्मीं ध्यात्वेति मन्त्रेण स भवेत्पृथिवीपतिः ॥
मातुलुङ्गं गदां खेटं पाणौ पात्रं च बिभ्रती ।
नागं लिङ्गं च योनिं च बिभ्रतीं चैव मूर्धनि ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pītavastrāṃ suvarṇāṅgīṃ padmahastadvāyānvitām ।
lakṣmīṃ dhyātvēti mantrēṇa sa bhavētpṛthivīpatiḥ ॥
mātuluṅgaṃ gadāṃ khēṭaṃ pāṇau pātraṃ cha bibhratī ।
nāgaṃ liṅgaṃ cha yōniṃ cha bibhratīṃ chaiva mūrdhani ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever meditates on Lakshmi — dressed in yellow, of golden form, holding two lotus flowers — with this mantra, shall become lord of the earth. She also holds a citron fruit, a mace, a shield, a vessel in her hands, along with a serpent, a linga and a yoni.

contentBlocks.original:
इति ध्यात्वा मानसोपचारैः सम्पूज्य ।
शङ्खचक्रगदाहस्ते शुभ्रवर्णे सुवासिनी ।
मम देहि वरं लक्ष्मीः सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनी ।
इति सम्प्रार्थ्य ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं ऐं महालक्ष्म्यै कमलधारिण्यै सिंहवाहिन्यै स्वाहा इति मन्त्रं जप्त्वा पुनः पूर्ववद्धृदयादि षडङ्गन्यासं कृत्वा स्तोत्रं पठेत् । ]
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti dhyātvā mānasōpachāraiḥ sampūjya ।
śaṅkhachakragadāhastē śubhravarṇē suvāsinī ।
mama dēhi varaṃ lakṣmīḥ sarvasiddhipradāyinī ।
iti samprārthya ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ aiṃ mahālakṣmyai kamaladhāriṇyai siṃhavāhinyai svāhā iti mantraṃ japtvā punaḥ pūrvavaddhṛdayādi ṣaḍaṅganyāsaṃ kṛtvā stōtraṃ paṭhēt । ]
contentBlocks.translation: Having thus meditated and worshipped with mental offerings, one prays: "O Lakshmi, holding conch, discus and mace, of white radiance, ever auspicious, bestower of all accomplishments — grant me a boon." Having offered this prayer: Om Shrim Hrim Aim, salutations to Mahalakshmi Kamalalaye.

contentBlocks.original:
स्तोत्रम् ।
वन्दे लक्ष्मीं परमशिवमयीं शुद्धजाम्बूनदाभां
तेजोरूपां कनकवसनां सर्वभूषोज्ज्वलाङ्गीम् ।
बीजापूरं कनककलशं हेमपद्मं दधाना-
-माद्यां शक्तिं सकलजननीं विष्णुवामाङ्कसंस्थाम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stōtram ।
vandē lakṣmīṃ paramaśivamayīṃ śuddhajāmbūnadābhāṃ
tējōrūpāṃ kanakavasanāṃ sarvabhūṣōjjvalāṅgīm ।
bījāpūraṃ kanakakalaśaṃ hēmapadmaṃ dadhānā-
-mādyāṃ śaktiṃ sakalajananīṃ viṣṇuvāmāṅkasaṃsthām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Stotra begins. I bow to Lakshmi, who is of pure Shiva-nature, radiant like refined gold, of luminous form, dressed in golden garments, adorned with all ornaments, holding a citron fruit and a golden vessel, and a golden lotus — the primal Shakti, mother of all, beloved of Vishnu. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमत्सौभाग्यजननीं स्तौमि लक्ष्मीं सनातनीम् ।
सर्वकामफलावाप्तिसाधनैकसुखावहाम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmatsaubhāgyajananīṃ staumi lakṣmīṃ sanātanīm ।
sarvakāmaphalāvāptisādhanaikasukhāvahām ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I praise the eternal Lakshmi, the bestower of supreme good fortune, the one who brings the attainment of all desired fruits and the sole source of lasting happiness. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
स्मरामि नित्यं देवेशि त्वया प्रेरितमानसः ।
त्वदाज्ञां शिरसा धृत्वा भजामि परमेश्वरीम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
smarāmi nityaṃ dēvēśi tvayā prēritamānasaḥ ।
tvadājñāṃ śirasā dhṛtvā bhajāmi paramēśvarīm ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I always remember you, O Goddess — inspired in my mind by you. Holding your command upon my head, I worship you, O Supreme Goddess. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तसम्पत्सुखदां महाश्रियं
समस्तसौभाग्यकरीं महाश्रियम् ।
समस्तकल्याणकरीं महाश्रियं
भजाम्यहं ज्ञानकरीं महाश्रियम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastasampatsukhadāṃ mahāśriyaṃ
samastasaubhāgyakarīṃ mahāśriyam ।
samastakaḻyāṇakarīṃ mahāśriyaṃ
bhajāmyahaṃ jñānakarīṃ mahāśriyam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the great Sri — bestower of all happiness and prosperity, maker of all good fortune, bringer of all auspiciousness, giver of all knowledge. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
विज्ञानसम्पत्सुखदां सनातनीं
विचित्रवाग्भूतिकरीं मनोहराम् ।
अनन्तसम्मोदसुखप्रदायिनीं
नमाम्यहं भूतिकरीं हरिप्रियाम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vijñānasampatsukhadāṃ sanātanīṃ
vichitravāgbhūtikarīṃ manōharām ।
anantasammōdasukhapradāyinīṃ
namāmyahaṃ bhūtikarīṃ haripriyām ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the eternal one — bestower of the happiness of spiritual wisdom, giver of the boon of manifold eloquence, enchanting to the mind, bestower of endless joy and happiness, dear to Hari. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तभूतान्तरसंस्थिता त्वं
समस्तभोक्त्रीश्वरि विश्वरूपे ।
तन्नास्ति यत्त्वद्व्यतिरिक्तवस्तु
त्वत्पादपद्मं प्रणमाम्यहं श्रीः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastabhūtāntarasaṃsthitā tvaṃ
samastabhōktrīśvari viśvarūpē ।
tannāsti yattvadvyatiriktavastu
tvatpādapadmaṃ praṇamāmyahaṃ śrīḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You dwell within all beings, O Goddess of the universe. There is nothing that exists apart from you. I bow to your lotus feet, O Sri. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
दारिद्र्य दुःखौघतमोपहन्त्री
त्वत्पादपद्मं मयि सन्निधत्स्व ।
दीनार्तिविच्छेदनहेतुभूतैः
कृपाकटाक्षैरभिषिञ्च मां श्रीः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dāridrya duḥkhaughatamōpahantrī
tvatpādapadmaṃ mayi sannidhatsva ।
dīnārtivichChēdanahētubhūtaiḥ
kṛpākaṭākṣairabhiṣiñcha māṃ śrīḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O destroyer of the deep darkness of poverty and suffering, place your lotus feet in my presence. Shower me with your compassionate glances, which cut away the afflictions of the humble. O Sri, consecrate me. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
अम्ब प्रसीद करुणासुधयार्द्रदृष्ट्या
मां त्वत्कृपाद्रविणगेहमिमं कुरुष्व ।
आलोकय प्रणतहृद्गतशोकहन्त्री
त्वत्पादपद्मयुगलं प्रणमाम्यहं श्रीः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amba prasīda karuṇāsudhayārdradṛṣṭyā
māṃ tvatkṛpādraviṇagēhamimaṃ kuruṣva ।
ālōkaya praṇatahṛdgataśōkahantrī
tvatpādapadmayugaḻaṃ praṇamāmyahaṃ śrīḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, be gracious — with your nectar-like gaze of compassion, make this body of mine a home of your grace-bestowing riches. O destroyer of the grief lodged in the hearts of the surrendered — I bow to your twin lotus feet, O Sri. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
शान्त्यै नमोऽस्तु शरणागतरक्षणायै
कान्त्यै नमोऽस्तु कमनीयगुणाश्रयायै ।
क्षान्त्यै नमोऽस्तु दुरितक्षयकारणायै
दात्र्यै नमोऽस्तु धनधान्यसमृद्धिदायै ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāntyai namō'stu śaraṇāgatarakṣaṇāyai
kāntyai namō'stu kamanīyaguṇāśrayāyai ।
kṣāntyai namō'stu duritakṣayakāraṇāyai
dātryai namō'stu dhanadhānyasamṛddhidāyai ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Shanti (peace), who protects the surrendered. Salutations to the one-pointed one, abode of beautiful qualities. Salutations to Kshanti (patience), cause of the dissolution of sin. Salutations to the giver of wealth and grain and prosperity. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
शक्त्यै नमोऽस्तु शशिशेखरसंस्तुतायै
रत्यै नमोऽस्तु रजनीकरसोदरायै ।
भक्त्यै नमोऽस्तु भवसागरतारकायै
मत्यै नमोऽस्तु मधुसूदनवल्लभायै ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaktyai namō'stu śaśiśēkharasaṃstutāyai
ratyai namō'stu rajanīkarasōdarāyai ।
bhaktyai namō'stu bhavasāgaratārakāyai
matyai namō'stu madhusūdanavallabhāyai ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Shakti, praised by Shiva. Salutations to Rati, sister of the moon. Salutations to Bhakti, the one who ferries across the ocean of existence. Salutations to Mati (wisdom), the beloved of Madhusudana. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्म्यै नमोऽस्तु शुभलक्षणलक्षितायै
सिद्ध्यै नमोऽस्तु शिवसिद्धसुपूजितायै ।
धृत्यै नमोऽस्त्वमितदुर्गतिभञ्जनायै
गत्यै नमोऽस्तु वरसद्गतिदायिकायै ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmyai namō'stu śubhalakṣaṇalakṣitāyai
siddhyai namō'stu śivasiddhasupūjitāyai ।
dhṛtyai namō'stvamitadurgatibhañjanāyai
gatyai namō'stu varasadgatidāyikāyai ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Lakshmi, marked with auspicious signs. Salutations to Siddhi, worshipped by perfected beings and Shiva. Salutations to Dhriti (fortitude), destroyer of boundless misfortune. Salutations to Gati (liberation), the one who bestows the supreme good. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
देव्यै नमोऽस्तु दिवि देवगणार्चितायै
भूत्यै नमोऽस्तु भुवनार्तिविनाशनायै ।
धात्र्यै नमोऽस्तु धरणीधरवल्लभायै
पुष्ट्यै नमोऽस्तु पुरुषोत्तमवल्लभायै ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvyai namō'stu divi dēvagaṇārchitāyai
bhūtyai namō'stu bhuvanārtivināśanāyai ।
dhātryai namō'stu dharaṇīdharavallabhāyai
puṣṭyai namō'stu puruṣōttamavallabhāyai ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the Devi, worshipped by the hosts of gods in heaven. Salutations to Bhuti (prosperity), destroyer of the world's suffering. Salutations to Dhatri, the beloved of the mountain-bearer (Vishnu). Salutations to Pushti (nourishment), the beloved of the Supreme Person. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
सुतीव्रदारिद्र्यविदुःखहन्त्र्यै
नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वभयापहन्त्र्यै ।
श्रीविष्णुवक्षःस्थलसंस्थितायै
नमो नमः सर्वविभूतिदायै ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sutīvradāridryaviduḥkhahantryai
namō'stu tē sarvabhayāpahantryai ।
śrīviṣṇuvakṣaḥsthalasaṃsthitāyai
namō namaḥ sarvavibhūtidāyai ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Destroyer of the intense suffering of terrible poverty — salutations to you, O remover of all fear. O you who dwell on the breast of Sri Vishnu — salutations again and again, O bestower of all prosperity. (13)

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु लक्ष्मीर्लक्षणालङ्कृताङ्गी
जयतु जयतु पद्मा पद्मसद्माभिवन्द्या ।
जयतु जयतु विद्या विष्णुवामाङ्कसंस्था
जयतु जयतु सम्यक्सर्वसम्पत्करी श्रीः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu lakṣmīrlakṣaṇālaṅkṛtāṅgī
jayatu jayatu padmā padmasadmābhivandyā ।
jayatu jayatu vidyā viṣṇuvāmāṅkasaṃsthā
jayatu jayatu samyaksarvasampatkarī śrīḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Victory, victory to Lakshmi, whose limbs are adorned with all auspicious marks! Victory to Padma, adored at the abode of the lotus! Victory to Vidya (knowledge), who dwells on Vishnu's left side! Victory to Sri, the true bestower of all wealth and prosperity! (14)

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु देवी देवसङ्घाभिपूज्या
जयतु जयतु भद्रा भार्गवी भाग्यरूपा ।
जयतु जयतु नित्या निर्मलज्ञानवेद्या
जयतु जयतु सत्या सर्वभूतान्तरस्था ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu dēvī dēvasaṅghābhipūjyā
jayatu jayatu bhadrā bhārgavī bhāgyarūpā ।
jayatu jayatu nityā nirmalajñānavēdyā
jayatu jayatu satyā sarvabhūtāntarasthā ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Victory to the Goddess, worshipped by all the hosts of gods! Victory to the auspicious one, to Bhargavi, the embodiment of good fortune! Victory to the eternal one, to be known through pure knowledge! Victory to the true one, who dwells within all beings! (15)

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु रम्या रत्नगर्भान्तरस्था
जयतु जयतु शुद्धा शुद्धजाम्बूनदाभा ।
जयतु जयतु कान्ता कान्तिमद्भासिताङ्गी
जयतु जयतु शान्ता शीघ्रमागच्छ सौम्ये ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu ramyā ratnagarbhāntarasthā
jayatu jayatu śuddhā śuddhajāmbūnadābhā ।
jayatu jayatu kāntā kāntimadbhāsitāṅgī
jayatu jayatu śāntā śīghramāgachCha saumyē ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Victory to the beautiful one, who dwells within the jewelled ocean! Victory to the pure one, radiant like refined gold! Victory to the beloved one, her form ablaze with beauty! Victory to the tranquil one — come quickly, O gentle one! (16)

contentBlocks.original:
यस्याः कलायाः कमलोद्भवाद्या
रुद्राश्च शक्र प्रमुखाश्च देवाः ।
जीवन्ति सर्वेऽपि सशक्तयस्ते
प्रभुत्वमाप्ताः परमायुषस्ते ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyāḥ kalāyāḥ kamalōdbhavādyā
rudrāścha śakra pramukhāścha dēvāḥ ।
jīvanti sarvē'pi saśaktayastē
prabhutvamāptāḥ paramāyuṣastē ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By whose divine ray Brahma, Rudra and all the gods headed by Indra live together with their powers, having obtained sovereignty and full lifespans. (17)

contentBlocks.original:
लिलेख निटिले विधिर्मम लिपिं विसृज्यान्तरं
त्वया विलिखितव्यमेतदिति तत्फलप्राप्तये ।
तदन्तरफलेस्फुटं कमलवासिनी श्रीरिमां
समर्पय समुद्रिकां सकलभाग्यसंसूचिकाम् ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lilēkha niṭilē vidhirmama lipiṃ visṛjyāntaraṃ
tvayā vilikhitavyamētaditi tatphalaprāptayē ।
tadantaraphalēsphuṭaṃ kamalavāsinī śrīrimāṃ
samarpaya samudrikāṃ sakalabhāgyasaṃsūchikām ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Brahma inscribed my destiny on my forehead, leaving space for you to write therein. O Lakshmi who dwells in the lotus, inscribe for me now the mark of all prosperity, this auspicious sign that reveals one's complete fortune. (18)

contentBlocks.original:
कलया ते यथा देवि जीवन्ति सचराचराः ।
तथा सम्पत्करे लक्ष्मि सर्वदा सम्प्रसीद मे ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalayā tē yathā dēvi jīvanti sacharācharāḥ ।
tathā sampatkarē lakṣmi sarvadā samprasīda mē ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, as all moving and unmoving beings live by your ray, so too, O Lakshmi, the bestower of wealth — always be gracious to me. (19)

contentBlocks.original:
यथा विष्णुर्ध्रुवे नित्यं स्वकलां सन्न्यवेशयत् ।
तथैव स्वकलां लक्ष्मि मयि सम्यक् समर्पय ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā viṣṇurdhruvē nityaṃ svakalāṃ sannyavēśayat ।
tathaiva svakalāṃ lakṣmi mayi samyak samarpaya ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As Vishnu permanently placed his divine ray in Dhruva — so too, O Lakshmi, properly place your divine ray in me. (20)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वसौख्यप्रदे देवि भक्तानामभयप्रदे ।
अचलां कुरु यत्नेन कलां मयि निवेशिताम् ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvasaukhyapradē dēvi bhaktānāmabhayapradē ।
achalāṃ kuru yatnēna kalāṃ mayi nivēśitām ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, bestower of all happiness, giver of fearlessness to devotees — establish your ray in me with care and make it unshakeable. (21)

contentBlocks.original:
मुदास्तां मत्फाले परमपदलक्ष्मीः स्फुटकला
सदा वैकुण्ठश्रीर्निवसतु कला मे नयनयोः ।
वसेत्सत्ये लोके मम वचसि लक्ष्मीर्वरकला
श्रियः श्वेतद्वीपे निवसतु कला मे स्वकरयोः ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mudāstāṃ matphālē paramapadalakṣmīḥ sphuṭakalā
sadā vaikuṇṭhaśrīrnivasatu kalā mē nayanayōḥ ।
vasētsatyē lōkē mama vachasi lakṣmīrvarakalā
śriyaḥ śvētadvīpē nivasatu kalā mē svakarayōḥ ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the Lakshmi of the supreme abode dwell visibly on my forehead; may the Sri of Vaikuntha always reside as a ray in my eyes; may Lakshmi dwell as a ray in the true world on my tongue; may the ray of Sri of the White Island dwell in my own hands. (22)

contentBlocks.original:
तावन्नित्यं ममाङ्गेषु क्षीराब्धौ श्रीकला वसेत् ।
सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ यावद्यावल्लक्ष्मीपतिः श्रियाः ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tāvannityaṃ mamāṅgēṣu kṣīrābdhau śrīkalā vasēt ।
sūryāchandramasau yāvadyāvallakṣmīpatiḥ śriyāḥ ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As long as there are sun and moon, as long as the lord of Lakshmi (Vishnu) exists — for that long may the ray of Sri dwell in my limbs in the ocean of milk. (23)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमङ्गलसम्पूर्णा सर्वैश्वर्यसमन्विता ।
आद्यादि श्रीर्महालक्ष्मी त्वत्कला मयि तिष्ठतु ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvamaṅgaḻasampūrṇā sarvaiśvaryasamanvitā ।
ādyādi śrīrmahālakṣmī tvatkalā mayi tiṣṭhatu ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Full of all auspiciousness, endowed with all sovereignty — may the ray of the primal Mahalakshmi dwell in me. (24)

contentBlocks.original:
अज्ञानतिमिरं हन्तुं शुद्धज्ञानप्रकाशिका ।
सर्वैश्वर्यप्रदा मेऽस्तु त्वत्कला मयि संस्थिता ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajñānatimiraṃ hantuṃ śuddhajñānaprakāśikā ।
sarvaiśvaryapradā mē'stu tvatkalā mayi saṃsthitā ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: To destroy the darkness of ignorance, as the illuminator of pure knowledge, the bestower of all sovereignty — may your ray, established in me, be mine. (25)

contentBlocks.original:
अलक्ष्मीं हरतु क्षिप्रं तमः सूर्यप्रभा यथा ।
वितनोतु मम श्रेयस्त्वत्कला मयि संस्थिता ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
alakṣmīṃ haratu kṣipraṃ tamaḥ sūryaprabhā yathā ।
vitanōtu mama śrēyastvatkaḻā mayi saṃsthitā ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As the sun's radiance swiftly destroys darkness — may your ray, established in me, similarly destroy poverty and extend my welfare. (26)

contentBlocks.original:
ऐश्वर्यमङ्गलोत्पत्तिस्त्वत्कलायां निधीयते ।
मयि तस्मात्कृतार्थोऽस्मि पात्रमस्मि स्थितेस्तव ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aiśvaryamaṅgaḻōtpattistvatkalāyāṃ nidhīyatē ।
mayi tasmātkṛtārthō'smi pātramasmi sthitēstava ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In your ray, sovereignty and auspiciousness find their home. Therefore, because of you, I am fulfilled; I am a worthy vessel for your dwelling. (27)

contentBlocks.original:
भवदावेशभाग्यार्हो भाग्यवानस्मि भार्गवि ।
त्वत्प्रसादात्पवित्रोऽहं लोकमातर्नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavadāvēśabhāgyārhō bhāgyavānasmi bhārgavi ।
tvatprasādātpavitrō'haṃ lōkamātarnamō'stu tē ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I am worthy of the fortune of your indwelling, O Bhargavi. I am blessed. By your grace I am purified, O Mother of the world — salutations to you. (28)

contentBlocks.original:
पुनासि मां त्वत्कलयैव यस्मा-
-दतः समागच्छ ममाग्रतस्त्वम् ।
परं पदं श्रीर्भव सुप्रसन्ना
मय्यच्युतेन प्रविशादिलक्ष्मीः ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
punāsi māṃ tvatkalayaiva yasmā-
-dataḥ samāgachCha mamāgratastvam ।
paraṃ padaṃ śrīrbhava suprasannā
mayyachyutēna praviśādilakṣmīḥ ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You purify me by your ray alone — therefore come before me. O Sri, the primal Lakshmi, be supremely gracious; enter with Achyuta (Vishnu) into me. (29)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीवैकुण्ठस्थिते लक्ष्मि समागच्छ ममाग्रतः ।
नारायणेन सह मां कृपादृष्ट्याऽवलोकय ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīvaikuṇṭhasthitē lakṣmi samāgachCha mamāgrataḥ ।
nārāyaṇēna saha māṃ kṛpādṛṣṭyā'valōkaya ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi who dwells in Sri Vaikuntha — come before me. Together with Narayana, behold me with your compassionate gaze. (30)

contentBlocks.original:
सत्यलोकस्थिते लक्ष्मि त्वं ममागच्छ सन्निधिम् ।
वासुदेवेन सहिता प्रसीद वरदा भव ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
satyalōkasthitē lakṣmi tvaṃ mamāgachCha sannidhim ।
vāsudēvēna sahitā prasīda varadā bhava ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi who dwells in Satyaloka — come into my presence. Together with Vasudeva, be pleased and become a boon-giver to me. (31)

contentBlocks.original:
श्वेतद्वीपस्थिते लक्ष्मि शीघ्रमागच्छ सुव्रते ।
विष्णुना सहिते देवि जगन्मातः प्रसीद मे ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śvētadvīpasthitē lakṣmi śīghramāgachCha suvratē ।
viṣṇunā sahitē dēvi jaganmātaḥ prasīda mē ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi who dwells in the White Island — quickly come, O keeper of good vows. O Goddess together with Vishnu, O mother of the universe, be gracious to me. (32)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षीराम्बुधिस्थिते लक्ष्मि समागच्छ समाधवा ।
त्वत्कृपादृष्टिसुधया सततं मां विलोकय ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣīrāmbudhisthitē lakṣmi samāgachCha samādhavā ।
tvatkṛpādṛṣṭisudhayā satataṃ māṃ vilōkaya ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi who dwells in the ocean of milk — come, together with Madhava. Behold me always with the nectar of your compassionate gaze. (33)

contentBlocks.original:
रत्नगर्भस्थिते लक्ष्मि परिपूर्णे हिरण्मये ।
समागच्छ समागच्छ स्थित्वाऽऽशु पुरतो मम ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ratnagarbhasthitē lakṣmi paripūrṇē hiraṇmayē ।
samāgachCha samāgachCha sthitvā''śu puratō mama ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi who dwells in the womb of jewels, in the golden fullness — come, come! Stand quickly before me. (34)

contentBlocks.original:
स्थिरा भव महालक्ष्मि निश्चला भव निर्मले ।
प्रसन्ने कमले देवि प्रसन्नहृदया भव ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sthirā bhava mahālakṣmi niśchalā bhava nirmalē ।
prasannē kamalē dēvi prasannahṛdayā bhava ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Be steady, O Mahalakshmi; be unwavering, O pure one. O gracious one, O Kamala, O Goddess — be joyful of heart. (35)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीधरे श्रीमहाभूते त्वदन्तःस्थं महानिधिम् ।
शीघ्रमुद्धृत्य पुरतः प्रदर्शय समर्पय ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīdharē śrīmahābhūtē tvadantaḥsthaṃ mahānidhim ।
śīghramuddhṛtya purataḥ pradarśaya samarpaya ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Sri, O great being, O bearer of all wealth — quickly draw out and place before me the great treasure that lies within you. (36)

contentBlocks.original:
वसुन्धरे श्रीवसुधे वसुदोग्ध्रि कृपामये ।
त्वत्कुक्षिगतसर्वस्वं शीघ्रं मे सम्प्रदर्शय ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vasundharē śrīvasudhē vasudōgdhri kṛpāmayē ।
tvatkukṣigatasarvasvaṃ śīghraṃ mē sampradarśaya ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Vasundhara (earth), O Sri Vasudhe (earth-goddess), O compassionate giver of riches — quickly show me all that you hold within your womb. (37)

contentBlocks.original:
विष्णुप्रिये रत्नगर्भे समस्तफलदे शिवे ।
त्वद्गर्भगतहेमादीन् सम्प्रदर्शय दर्शय ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viṣṇupriyē ratnagarbhē samastaphaladē śivē ।
tvadgarbhagatahēmādīn sampradarśaya darśaya ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O beloved of Vishnu, O womb of jewels, O bestower of all fruits, O auspicious one — show me, show me the gold and other treasures within your womb. (38)

contentBlocks.original:
रसातलगते लक्ष्मि शीघ्रमागच्छ मे पुरः ।
न जाने परमं रूपं मातर्मे सम्प्रदर्शय ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rasātalagatē lakṣmi śīghramāgachCha mē puraḥ ।
na jānē paramaṃ rūpaṃ mātarmē sampradarśaya ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi who dwells in the netherworld — quickly come before me. I do not know your supreme form, O Mother — reveal it to me. (39)

contentBlocks.original:
आविर्भव मनोवेगाच्छीघ्रमागच्छ मे पुरः ।
मा वत्स भैरिहेत्युक्त्वा कामं गौरिव रक्ष माम् ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āvirbhava manōvēgāchChīghramāgachCha mē puraḥ ।
mā vatsa bhairihētyuktvā kāmaṃ gauriva rakṣa mām ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Manifest at the speed of thought — come quickly before me. Saying "Fear not, my child — I have placed the gift of fearlessness upon you," O compassionate one toward the humble, protect me like a cow her calf. (40)

contentBlocks.original:
देवि शीघ्रं समागच्छ धरणीगर्भसंस्थिते ।
मातस्त्वद्भृत्यभृत्योऽहं मृगये त्वां कुतूहलात् ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvi śīghraṃ samāgachCha dharaṇīgarbhasaṃsthitē ।
mātastvadbhṛtyabhṛtyō'haṃ mṛgayē tvāṃ kutūhalāt ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, O Dharanigarbha (womb of the earth) — come quickly. O Mother, I am your servant's servant; I seek you with longing. (41)

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तिष्ठ जागृहि त्वं मे समुत्तिष्ठ सुजागृहि ।
अक्षयान् हेमकलशान् सुवर्णेन सुपूरितान् ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttiṣṭha jāgṛhi tvaṃ mē samuttiṣṭha sujāgṛhi ।
akṣayān hēmakalaśān suvarṇēna supūritān ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Arise and wake for me; arise well and fully wake. Inexhaustible golden vessels filled abundantly with gold — (42)

contentBlocks.original:
निक्षेपान्मे समाकृष्य समुद्धृत्य ममाग्रतः ।
समुन्नतानना भूत्वा समाधेहि धरान्तरात् ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nikṣēpānmē samākṛṣya samuddhṛtya mamāgrataḥ ।
samunnatānanā bhūtvā samādhēhi dharāntarāt ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — drawing these treasures out and lifting them, place them before me; rise with uplifted face and install them here from within the earth. (43)

contentBlocks.original:
मत्सन्निधिं समागच्छ मदाहितकृपारसात् ।
प्रसीद श्रेयसां दोग्ध्री लक्ष्मीर्मे नयनाग्रतः ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
matsannidhiṃ samāgachCha madāhitakṛpārasāt ।
prasīda śrēyasāṃ dōgdhrī lakṣmīrmē nayanāgrataḥ ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Come into my presence from the nectar of your compassion for me. O Lakshmi, be pleased, O milch-cow of all that is good — stand before my eyes. (44)

contentBlocks.original:
अत्रोपविश लक्ष्मि त्वं स्थिरा भव हिरण्मये ।
सुस्थिरा भव सम्प्रीत्या प्रसीद वरदा भव ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atrōpaviśa lakṣmi tvaṃ sthirā bhava hiraṇmayē ।
susthirā bhava samprītyā prasīda varadā bhava ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sit here, O Lakshmi, in your golden form; be steady and firm; be joyfully pleased and become a boon-giver to me. (45)

contentBlocks.original:
आनीतांस्तु तथा देवि निधीन्मे सम्प्रदर्शय ।
अद्य क्षणेन सहसा दत्त्वा संरक्ष मां सदा ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānītāṃstu tathā dēvi nidhīnmē sampradarśaya ।
adya kṣaṇēna sahasā dattvā saṃrakṣa māṃ sadā ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Show me the treasures that have been brought here, O Goddess. Give them this very instant, at once, and protect me always. (46)

contentBlocks.original:
मयि तिष्ठ तथा नित्यं यथेन्द्रादिषु तिष्ठसि ।
अभयं कुरु मे देवि महालक्ष्मीर्नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mayi tiṣṭha tathā nityaṃ yathēndrādiṣu tiṣṭhasi ।
abhayaṃ kuru mē dēvi mahālakṣmīrnamō'stu tē ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dwell in me always, just as you dwell in Indra and others. Grant me fearlessness, O Goddess — Mahalakshmi, salutations to you. (47)

contentBlocks.original:
समागच्छ महालक्ष्मि शुद्धजाम्बूनदप्रभे ।
प्रसीद पुरतः स्थित्वा प्रणतं मां विलोकय ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samāgachCha mahālakṣmi śuddhajāmbūnadaprabhē ।
prasīda purataḥ sthitvā praṇataṃ māṃ vilōkaya ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Come, O Mahalakshmi, of the pure radiance of refined gold. Be pleased and standing before me, behold me who bows to you. (48)

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीर्भुवं गता भासि यत्र यत्र हिरण्मयी ।
तत्र तत्र स्थिता त्वं मे तव रूपं प्रदर्शय ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīrbhuvaṃ gatā bhāsi yatra yatra hiraṇmayī ।
tatra tatra sthitā tvaṃ mē tava rūpaṃ pradarśaya ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi, wherever you manifest on earth in your golden form — there you dwell; in all those places, show me your form. (49)

contentBlocks.original:
क्रीडन्ती बहुधा भूमौ परिपूर्णकृपामयि ।
मम मूर्धनि ते हस्तमविलम्बितमर्पय ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
krīḍantī bahudhā bhūmau paripūrṇakṛpāmayi ।
mama mūrdhani tē hastamavilambitamarpaya ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O overflowing with compassion, playing in many ways on the earth — without delay, place your hand upon my head. (50)

contentBlocks.original:
फलद्भाग्योदये लक्ष्मि समस्तपुरवासिनी ।
प्रसीद मे महालक्ष्मि परिपूर्णमनोरथे ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phaladbhāgyōdayē lakṣmi samastapuravāsinī ।
prasīda mē mahālakṣmi paripūrṇamanōrathē ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi, who dwells in all towns at the dawn of the fruition of fortune — be gracious to me, O Mahalakshmi, in the fulfilment of all my desires. (51)

contentBlocks.original:
अयोध्यादिषु सर्वेषु नगरेषु समास्थिते ।
वैभवैर्विविधैर्युक्तैः समागच्छ मुदान्विते ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ayōdhyādiṣu sarvēṣu nagarēṣu samāsthitē ।
vaibhavairvividhairyuktaiḥ samāgachCha mudānvitē ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you who are present in Ayodhya and all other cities, endowed with various glories — come joyfully to me. (52)

contentBlocks.original:
समागच्छ समागच्छ ममाग्रे भव सुस्थिरा ।
करुणारसनिष्यन्दनेत्रद्वय विलासिनी ॥ 53 ॥ [निष्पन्न]
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samāgachCha samāgachCha mamāgrē bhava susthirā ।
karuṇārasaniṣyandanētradvaya vilāsinī ॥ 53 ॥ [niṣpanna]
contentBlocks.translation: Come, come; stand steadily before me. O you whose two eyes stream with the nectar of compassion. (53)

contentBlocks.original:
सन्निधत्स्व महालक्ष्मि त्वत्पाणिं मम मस्तके ।
करुणासुधया मां त्वमभिषिञ्च्य स्थिरं कुरु ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sannidhatsva mahālakṣmi tvatpāṇiṃ mama mastakē ।
karuṇāsudhayā māṃ tvamabhiṣiñchya sthiraṃ kuru ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mahalakshmi, be present; place your hand on my head. Consecrate me with the nectar of your compassion and make me steadfast. (54)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वराजगृहे लक्ष्मि समागच्छ बलान्विते । [मुदान्विते]
स्थित्वाऽऽशु पुरतो मेऽद्य प्रसादेनाऽभयं कुरु ॥ 55 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvarājagṛhē lakṣmi samāgachCha balānvitē । [mudānvitē]
sthitvā''śu puratō mē'dya prasādēnā'bhayaṃ kuru ॥ 55 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi, who dwells in all royal palaces, endowed with power — standing before me this very day, by your grace grant me fearlessness. (55)

contentBlocks.original:
सादरं मस्तके हस्तं मम त्वं कृपयार्पय ।
सर्वराजगृहे लक्ष्मि त्वत्कला मयि तिष्ठतु ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sādaraṃ mastakē hastaṃ mama tvaṃ kṛpayārpaya ।
sarvarājagṛhē lakṣmi tvatkalā mayi tiṣṭhatu ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Lovingly place your hand upon my head with compassion. O Lakshmi, who dwells in all royal palaces — may your ray dwell in me. (56)

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादि श्रीमहालक्ष्मि विष्णुवामाङ्कसंस्थिते ।
प्रत्यक्षं कुरु मे रूपं रक्ष मां शरणागतम् ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādi śrīmahālakṣmi viṣṇuvāmāṅkasaṃsthitē ।
pratyakṣaṃ kuru mē rūpaṃ rakṣa māṃ śaraṇāgatam ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O primal Mahalakshmi who dwells on the left side of Vishnu — make your form visible to me; protect me who has taken refuge. (57)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रसीद मे महालक्ष्मि सुप्रसीद महाशिवे ।
अचला भव सम्प्रीत्या सुस्थिरा भव मद्गृहे ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prasīda mē mahālakṣmi suprasīda mahāśivē ।
achalā bhava samprītyā susthirā bhava madgṛhē ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mahalakshmi, be gracious; be supremely gracious, O greatly auspicious one. Be unwavering, joyfully pleased; be steadfast in my home. (58)

contentBlocks.original:
यावत्तिष्ठन्ति वेदाश्च यावच्चन्द्रदिवाकरौ ।
यावद्विष्णुश्च यावत्त्वं तावत्कुरु कृपां मयि ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāvattiṣṭhanti vēdāścha yāvachchandradivākarau ।
yāvadviṣṇuścha yāvattvaṃ tāvatkuru kṛpāṃ mayi ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As long as the Vedas remain, as long as sun and moon exist, as long as Vishnu exists, as long as you exist — for that long show compassion to me. (59)

contentBlocks.original:
चान्द्रीकला यथा शुक्ले वर्धते सा दिने दिने ।
तथा दया ते मय्येव वर्धतामभिवर्धताम् ॥ 60 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chāndrīkalā yathā śuklē vardhatē sā dinē dinē ।
tathā dayā tē mayyēva vardhatāmabhivardhatām ॥ 60 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As the lunar ray grows day by day in the bright fortnight — so too may your compassion toward me grow and ever increase. (60)

contentBlocks.original:
यथा वैकुण्ठनगरे यथा वै क्षीरसागरे ।
तथा मद्भवने तिष्ठ स्थिरं श्रीविष्णुना सह ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā vaikuṇṭhanagarē yathā vai kṣīrasāgarē ।
tathā madbhavanē tiṣṭha sthiraṃ śrīviṣṇunā saha ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As you dwell in the city of Vaikuntha and in the ocean of milk — so too dwell in my home steadfastly together with Sri Vishnu. (61)

contentBlocks.original:
योगिनां हृदये नित्यं यथा तिष्ठसि विष्णुना ।
तथा मद्भवने तिष्ठ स्थिरं श्रीविष्णुना सह ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgināṃ hṛdayē nityaṃ yathā tiṣṭhasi viṣṇunā ।
tathā madbhavanē tiṣṭha sthiraṃ śrīviṣṇunā saha ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Just as you dwell eternally in the hearts of yogis together with Vishnu — so too dwell in my home steadfastly together with Sri Vishnu. (62)

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणस्य हृदये भवती यथास्ते
नारायणोऽपि तव हृत्कमले यथास्ते ।
नारायणस्त्वमपि नित्यमुभौ तथैव
तौ तिष्ठतां हृदि ममापि दयान्वितौ श्रीः ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇasya hṛdayē bhavatī yathāstē
nārāyaṇō'pi tava hṛtkamalē yathāstē ।
nārāyaṇastvamapi nityamubhau tathaiva
tau tiṣṭhatāṃ hṛdi mamāpi dayānvitau śrīḥ ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As you dwell in the heart of Narayana, and Narayana dwells in your heart-lotus, and both of you are eternal — may both of you, filled with compassion, dwell in my heart too, O Sri. (63)

contentBlocks.original:
विज्ञानवृद्धिं हृदये कुरु श्रीः
सौभाग्यवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः ।
दयासुवृद्धिं कुरुतां मयि श्रीः
सुवर्णवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vijñānavṛddhiṃ hṛdayē kuru śrīḥ
saubhāgyavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ ।
dayāsuvṛddhiṃ kurutāṃ mayi śrīḥ
suvarṇavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Sri, increase wisdom in my heart! O Sri, increase prosperity in my home! O Sri, may compassion grow greatly in me! O Sri, increase gold in my home! (64)

contentBlocks.original:
न मां त्यजेथाः श्रितकल्पवल्लि
सद्भक्तचिन्तामणिकामधेनो ।
विश्वस्य मातर्भव सुप्रसन्ना
गृहे कलत्रेषु च पुत्रवर्गे ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na māṃ tyajēthāḥ śritakalpavalli
sadbhaktachintāmaṇikāmadhēnō ।
viśvasya mātarbhava suprasannā
gṛhē kalatrēṣu cha putravargē ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Do not abandon me — O wish-granting creeper for the surrendered, O cintamani of devoted thought, O wish-fulfilling cow. Be gracious, O mother of the universe, in my home, among my family, wife and children. (65)

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिमाये त्वमजाण्डबीजं
त्वमेव साकारनिराकृतिस्त्वम् ।
त्वया धृताश्चाब्जभवाण्डसङ्घा-
-श्चित्रं चरित्रं तव देवि विष्णोः ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādimāyē tvamajāṇḍabījaṃ
tvamēva sākāranirākṛtistvam ।
tvayā dhṛtāśchābjabhavāṇḍasaṅghā-
-śchitraṃ charitraṃ tava dēvi viṣṇōḥ ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O primal Maya, you are the seed of the unmanifest (anda bija), you are both the manifest and the unmanifest form. By you are the multitudes of universes of Brahma sustained. Wondrous is the story of you and Vishnu, O Goddess. (66)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मरुद्रादयो देवा वेदाश्चापि न शक्नुयुः ।
महिमानं तव स्तोतुं मन्दोऽहं शक्नुयां कथम् ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmarudrādayō dēvā vēdāśchāpi na śaknuyuḥ ।
mahimānaṃ tava stōtuṃ mandō'haṃ śaknuyāṃ katham ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Not even Brahma, Rudra and the gods are able to praise your glory adequately. How then can I, who am simple-minded, be capable of it? (67)

contentBlocks.original:
अम्ब त्वद्वत्सवाक्यानि सूक्तासूक्तानि यानि च ।
तानि स्वीकुरु सर्वज्ञे दयालुत्वेन सादरम् ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amba tvadvatsavākyāni sūktāsūktāni yāni cha ।
tāni svīkuru sarvajñē dayālutvēna sādaram ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, these words of mine — appropriate or inappropriate — please accept them with compassion, O all-knowing one, with honour. (68)

contentBlocks.original:
भवतीं शरणं गत्वा कृतार्थाः स्युः पुरातनाः ।
इति सञ्चिन्त्य मनसा त्वामहं शरणं व्रजे ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavatīṃ śaraṇaṃ gatvā kṛtārthāḥ syuḥ purātanāḥ ।
iti sañchintya manasā tvāmahaṃ śaraṇaṃ vrajē ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those of ancient times who took refuge in you became fulfilled. Contemplating this in my mind, I too take refuge in you. (69)

contentBlocks.original:
अनन्ता नित्यसुखिनस्त्वद्भक्तास्त्वत्परायणाः ।
इति वेदप्रमाणाद्धि देवि त्वां शरणं व्रजे ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantā nityasukhinastvadbhaktāstvatparāyaṇāḥ ।
iti vēdapramāṇāddhi dēvi tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ vrajē ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Your devotees who are devoted to you are eternal and ever happy — thus, O Goddess, by the authority of the Vedas, I take refuge in you. (70)

contentBlocks.original:
तव प्रतिज्ञा मद्भक्ता न नश्यन्तीत्यपि क्वचित् ।
इति सञ्चिन्त्य सञ्चिन्त्य प्राणान् सन्धारयाम्यहम् ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava pratijñā madbhaktā na naśyantītyapi kvachit ।
iti sañchintya sañchintya prāṇān sandhārayāmyaham ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Your promise is that "my devotees shall never perish" — anywhere. Thinking on this again and again, I sustain my life. (71)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदधीनस्त्वहं मातस्त्वत्कृपा मयि विद्यते ।
यावत्सम्पूर्णकामः स्यात्तावद्देहि दयानिधे ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadadhīnastvahaṃ mātastvatkṛpā mayi vidyatē ।
yāvatsampūrṇakāmaḥ syāttāvaddēhi dayānidhē ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I am dependent on you, O Mother — your grace is upon me. O treasure of compassion, bestow upon me until all my desires are fully fulfilled. (72)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षणमात्रं न शक्नोमि जीवितुं त्वत्कृपां विना ।
न जीवन्तीह जलजा जलं त्यक्त्वा जलग्रहाः ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣaṇamātraṃ na śaknōmi jīvituṃ tvatkṛpāṃ vinā ।
na jīvantīha jalajā jalaṃ tyaktvā jalagrahāḥ ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I cannot live even for a moment without your grace. Aquatic creatures that dwell in water cannot live if the water is abandoned. (73)

contentBlocks.original:
यथा हि पुत्रवात्सल्याज्जननी प्रस्नुतस्तनी ।
वत्सं त्वरितमागत्य सम्प्रीणयति वत्सला ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā hi putravātsalyājjananī prasnutastanī ।
vatsaṃ tvaritamāgatya samprīṇayati vatsalā ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Just as a mother, overflowing with love for her child, comes swiftly with milk flowing from her breasts and lovingly nourishes her calf — (74)

contentBlocks.original:
यदि स्यां तव पुत्रोऽहं माता त्वं यदि मामकी ।
दयापयोधरस्तन्यसुधाभिरभिषिञ्च माम् ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadi syāṃ tava putrō'haṃ mātā tvaṃ yadi māmakī ।
dayāpayōdharastanyasudhābhirabhiṣiñcha mām ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: If I am your child and you are my mother — then consecrate me with the nectar flowing from the breasts of your ocean of compassion. (75)

contentBlocks.original:
मृग्यो न गुणलेशोऽपि मयि दोषैकमन्दिरे ।
पांसूनां वृष्टिबिन्दूनां दोषाणां च न मे मतिः ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛgyō na guṇalēśō'pi mayi dōṣaikamandirē ।
pāṃsūnāṃ vṛṣṭibindūnāṃ dōṣāṇāṃ cha na mē matiḥ ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Not even a trace of good qualities is to be found in me, who am a sole abode of faults. My mind does not count the faults as numerous as dust particles or raindrops. (76)

contentBlocks.original:
पापिनामहमेवाग्र्यो दयालूनां त्वमग्रणीः ।
दयनीयो मदन्योऽस्ति तव कोऽत्र जगत्त्रये ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpināmahamēvāgryō dayālūnāṃ tvamagraṇīḥ ।
dayanīyō madanyō'sti tava kō'tra jagattrayē ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I am the foremost among sinners; you are the foremost among the compassionate. Who other than me in all three worlds is more deserving of your compassion? (77)

contentBlocks.original:
विधिनाहं न सृष्टश्चेन्न स्यात्तव दयालुता ।
आमयो वा न सृष्टश्चेदौषधस्य वृथोदयः ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidhināhaṃ na sṛṣṭaśchēnna syāttava dayālutā ।
āmayō vā na sṛṣṭaśchēdauṣadhasya vṛthōdayaḥ ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: If Brahma had not created me, your quality of compassion would not exist. If disease had not been created, the arising of medicine would be purposeless. (78)

contentBlocks.original:
कृपा मदग्रजा किं ते अहं किं वा तदग्रजः ।
विचार्य देहि मे वित्तं तव देवि दयानिधे ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛpā madagrajā kiṃ tē ahaṃ kiṃ vā tadagrajaḥ ।
vichārya dēhi mē vittaṃ tava dēvi dayānidhē ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Does compassion precede me, or do I precede compassion? Considering this, O Goddess, O treasure of compassion — give me wealth. (79)

contentBlocks.original:
माता पिता त्वं गुरुसद्गतिः श्री-
-स्त्वमेव सञ्जीवनहेतुभूता ।
अन्यं न मन्ये जगदेकनाथे
त्वमेव सर्वं मम देवि सत्ये ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mātā pitā tvaṃ gurusadgatiḥ śrī-
-stvamēva sañjīvanahētubhūtā ।
anyaṃ na manyē jagadēkanāthē
tvamēva sarvaṃ mama dēvi satyē ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Sri — you are my mother, father, guru and good path; you are the very cause of giving life back to me. I consider no other lord of the universe. You are truly everything for me, O Goddess. (80)

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिलक्ष्मीर्भव सुप्रसन्ना
विशुद्धविज्ञानसुखैकदोग्ध्री ।
अज्ञानहन्त्री त्रिगुणातिरिक्ता
प्रज्ञाननेत्री भव सुप्रसन्ना ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādilakṣmīrbhava suprasannā
viśuddhavijñānasukhaikadōgdhrī ।
ajñānahantrī triguṇātiriktā
prajñānanētrī bhava suprasannā ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O primal Lakshmi, be supremely gracious — the sole milch-cow of the happiness of pure and transcendent wisdom. O destroyer of ignorance, beyond the three gunas, O illuminator of true knowledge — be supremely gracious. (81)

contentBlocks.original:
अशेषवाग्जाड्यमलापहन्त्री
नवं नवं स्पष्टसुवाक्प्रदायिनी ।
ममेह जिह्वाग्र सुरङ्गनर्तकी [नर्तिनी]
भव प्रसन्ना वदने च मे श्रीः ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aśēṣavāgjāḍyamalāpahantrī
navaṃ navaṃ spaṣṭasuvākpradāyinī ।
mamēha jihvāgra suraṅganartakī [nartinī]
bhava prasannā vadanē cha mē śrīḥ ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O destroyer of the dense impurity of all sluggishness of speech, O giver again and again of clear, excellent power of expression — O Sri, dance on the tip of my tongue like a celestial nymph, be gracious in my face. (82)

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तसम्पत्सुविराजमाना
समस्ततेजश्चयभासमाना ।
विष्णुप्रिये त्वं भव दीप्यमाना
वाग्देवता मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastasampatsuvirājamānā
samastatējaśchayabhāsamānā ।
viṣṇupriyē tvaṃ bhava dīpyamānā
vāgdēvatā mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O beloved of Vishnu — you, resplendent with all wealth and treasures, effulgent with all radiance and splendour — be manifested brightly; O goddess of speech, be gracious in my eye. (83)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वप्रदर्शे सकलार्थदे त्वं
प्रभासुलावण्यदयाप्रदोग्ध्री ।
सुवर्णदे त्वं सुमुखी भव श्री-
-र्हिरण्मयी मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvapradarśē sakalārthadē tvaṃ
prabhāsulāvaṇyadayāpradōgdhrī ।
suvarṇadē tvaṃ sumukhī bhava śrī-
-rhiraṇmayī mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you who reveal everything, O fulfiller of all purposes, O gracious one full of lustre and beauty — O bestower of gold, O beautiful-faced Sri, O golden one — be gracious in my eye. (84)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वार्थदा सर्वजगत्प्रसूतिः
सर्वेश्वरी सर्वभयापहन्त्री ।
सर्वोन्नता त्वं सुमुखी भव श्री-
-र्हिरण्मयी मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvārthadā sarvajagatprasūtiḥ
sarvēśvarī sarvabhayāpahantrī ।
sarvōnnatā tvaṃ sumukhī bhava śrī-
-rhiraṇmayī mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O fulfiller of all purposes, O mother of all the world, O supreme goddess, O destroyer of all fear — O supremely exalted Sri, be gracious — O golden one, be gracious in my eye. (85)

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तविघ्नौघविनाशकारिणी
समस्तभक्तोद्धरणे विचक्षणा ।
अनन्तसौभाग्यसुखप्रदायिनी
हिरण्मयी मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastavighnaughavināśakāriṇī
samastabhaktōddharaṇē vichakṣaṇā ।
anantasaubhāgyasukhapradāyinī
hiraṇmayī mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O destroyer of all multitudes of obstacles, O skillful one in rescuing all devotees, O bestower of endless good fortune and joy — O golden one, be gracious in my eye. (86)

contentBlocks.original:
देवि प्रसीद दयनीयतमाय मह्यं
देवाधिनाथभवदेवगणाभिवन्द्ये ।
मातस्तथैव भव सन्निहिता दृशोर्मे
पत्या समं मम मुखे भव सुप्रसन्ना ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvi prasīda dayanīyatamāya mahyaṃ
dēvādhināthabhavadēvagaṇābhivandyē ।
mātastathaiva bhava sannihitā dṛśōrmē
patyā samaṃ mama mukhē bhava suprasannā ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, be gracious to me who am the most pitiable; O you who are worshipped by the chief of gods and the lord of gods — O Mother, be present before my eyes; together with your lord, be supremely gracious in my face. (87)

contentBlocks.original:
मा वत्स भैरभयदानकरोऽर्पितस्ते
मौलौ ममेति मयि दीनदयानुकम्पे ।
मातः समर्पय मुदा करुणाकटाक्षं
माङ्गल्यबीजमिह नः सृज जन्म मातः ॥ 88 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mā vatsa bhairabhayadānakarō'rpitastē
maulau mamēti mayi dīnadayānukampē ।
mātaḥ samarpaya mudā karuṇākaṭākṣaṃ
māṅgaḻyabījamiha naḥ sṛja janma mātaḥ ॥ 88 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: "Fear not, my child — I have placed the gift of fearlessness upon your crown" — O Mother, compassionate toward the humble, joyfully bestow upon me your compassionate sidelong glance; here in this birth, O Mother, sow the seed of all auspiciousness. (88)

contentBlocks.original:
कटाक्ष इह कामधुक्तव मनस्तु चिन्तामणिः
करः सुरतरुः सदा नवनिधिस्त्वमेवेन्दिरे ।
भवे तव दयारसो मम रसायनं चान्वहं
मुखं तव कलानिधिर्विविधवाञ्छितार्थप्रदम् ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṭākṣa iha kāmadhuktava manastu chintāmaṇiḥ
karaḥ surataruḥ sadā navanidhistvamēvēndirē ।
bhavē tava dayārasō mama rasāyanaṃ chānvahaṃ
mukhaṃ tava kalānidhirvividhavāñChitārthapradam ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Your sidelong glance here is the wish-fulfilling cow; your mind is the cintamani jewel; your hand is the wish-granting tree; you yourself, O Indira, are always the nine treasures. Your nectar of compassion shall be my daily elixir; your face, the treasury of divine arts, bestows all desired purposes. (89)

contentBlocks.original:
यथा रसस्पर्शनतोऽयसोऽपि
सुवर्णता स्यात्कमले तथा ते ।
कटाक्षसंस्पर्शनतो जनाना-
-ममङ्गलानामपि मङ्गलत्वम् ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā rasasparśanatō'yasō'pi
suvarṇatā syātkamalē tathā tē ।
kaṭākṣasaṃsparśanatō janānā-
-mamaṅgaḻānāmapi maṅgaḻatvam ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Just as even iron is transformed into gold by the touch of the philosopher's stone — so too, by the touch of your sidelong glance, O Kamala, even the inauspicious become auspicious. (90)

contentBlocks.original:
देहीति नास्तीति वचः प्रवेशा-
-द्भीतो रमे त्वां शरणं प्रपद्ये ।
अतः सदाऽस्मिन्नभयप्रदा त्वं
सहैव पत्या मयि सन्निधेहि ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēhīti nāstīti vachaḥ pravēśā-
-dbhītō ramē tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ।
ataḥ sadā'sminnabhayapradā tvaṃ
sahaiva patyā mayi sannidhēhi ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Afraid of the words "give" and "there is none," O Rama, I take refuge in you. Therefore always be the fearless one for me here; dwell in me, together with your lord. (91)

contentBlocks.original:
कल्पद्रुमेण मणिना सहिता सुरम्या
श्रीस्ते कला मयि रसेन रसायनेन ।
आस्तां यतो मम शिरःकरदृष्टिपाद-
-स्पृष्टाः सुवर्णवपुषः स्थिरजङ्गमाः स्युः ॥ 92 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalpadrumēṇa maṇinā sahitā suramyā
śrīstē kalā mayi rasēna rasāyanēna ।
āstāṃ yatō mama śiraḥkaradṛṣṭipāda-
-spṛṣṭāḥ suvarṇavapuṣaḥ sthirajaṅgamāḥ syuḥ ॥ 92 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May your divine ray, together with the wish-granting tree and the philosopher's stone, beautiful Sri — by the nectar-essence of this ray — abide in me; so that by the touch of my head, hands, gaze and feet, all moving and unmoving things become golden in form. (92)

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिविष्णोः स्थिरधर्मपत्नी
त्वमेव पत्या मयि सन्निधेहि ।
आद्यादिलक्ष्मि त्वदनुग्रहेण
पदे पदे मे निधिदर्शनं स्यात् ॥ 93 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādiviṣṇōḥ sthiradharmapatnī
tvamēva patyā mayi sannidhēhi ।
ādyādilakṣmi tvadanugrahēṇa
padē padē mē nidhidarśanaṃ syāt ॥ 93 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O eternal consort of the primal Vishnu — dwell in me together with your lord. By your grace, O primal Lakshmi, may I encounter treasure at every step. (93)

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिलक्ष्मीहृदयं पठेद्यः
स राज्यलक्ष्मीमचलां तनोति ।
महादरिद्रोऽपि भवेद्धनाढ्य-
-स्तदन्वये श्रीः स्थिरतां प्रयाति ॥ 94 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādilakṣmīhṛdayaṃ paṭhēdyaḥ
sa rājyalakṣmīmachalāṃ tanōti ।
mahādaridrō'pi bhavēddhanāḍhya-
-stadanvayē śrīḥ sthiratāṃ prayāti ॥ 94 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites this Primal Lakshmi Hridayam, that person extends his royal fortune in a stable manner. Even the greatly destitute person becomes wealthy with riches, and in that lineage, Sri (prosperity) attains permanence. (94)

contentBlocks.original:
यस्य स्मरणमात्रेण तुष्टा स्याद्विष्णुवल्लभा ।
तस्याभीष्टं ददत्याशु तं पालयति पुत्रवत् ॥ 95 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasya smaraṇamātrēṇa tuṣṭā syādviṣṇuvallabhā ।
tasyābhīṣṭaṃ dadatyāśu taṃ pālayati putravat ॥ 95 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Merely by remembering which one, the beloved of Vishnu becomes pleased — she quickly bestows what he desires and protects him like a son. (95)

contentBlocks.original:
इदं रहस्यं हृदयं सर्वकामफलप्रदम् ।
जपः पञ्चसहस्रं तु पुरश्चरणमुच्यते ॥ 96 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ rahasyaṃ hṛdayaṃ sarvakāmaphalapradam ।
japaḥ pañchasahasraṃ tu puraścharaṇamuchyatē ॥ 96 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This secret Hridayam bestows the fruits of all desires. Reciting it five thousand times is said to be the purashcharana (formal completion). (96)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिकालमेककालं वा नरो भक्तिसमन्वितः ।
यः पठेच्छृणुयाद्वापि स याति परमां श्रियम् ॥ 97 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trikālamēkakālaṃ vā narō bhaktisamanvitaḥ ।
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyādvāpi sa yāti paramāṃ śriyam ॥ 97 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever, endowed with devotion, reads or hears this at all three times of day or even once — attains the supreme Sri (prosperity and liberation). (97)

contentBlocks.original:
महालक्ष्मीं समुद्दिश्य निशि भार्गववासरे ।
इदं श्रीहृदयं जप्त्वा पञ्चवारं धनी भवेत् ॥ 98 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahālakṣmīṃ samuddiśya niśi bhārgavavāsarē ।
idaṃ śrīhṛdayaṃ japtvā pañchavāraṃ dhanī bhavēt ॥ 98 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever, directing their practice to Mahalakshmi, recites this Sri Hridayam five times on Friday night — shall become wealthy. (98)

contentBlocks.original:
अनेन हृदयेनान्नं गर्भिण्या अभिमन्त्रितम् ।
ददाति तत्कुले पुत्रो जायते श्रीपतिः स्वयम् ॥ 99 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anēna hṛdayēnānnaṃ garbhiṇyā abhimantritam ।
dadāti tatkulē putrō jāyatē śrīpatiḥ svayam ॥ 99 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Food charged with this Hridayam mantra and given to a pregnant woman — in that family, Vishnu himself shall be born as a son. (99)

contentBlocks.original:
नरेण वाऽथवा नार्या लक्ष्मीहृदयमन्त्रिते ।
जले पीते च तद्वंशे मन्दभाग्यो न जायते ॥ 100 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
narēṇa vā'thavā nāryā lakṣmīhṛdayamantritē ।
jalē pītē cha tadvaṃśē mandabhāgyō na jāyatē ॥ 100 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whether drunk by a man or a woman, water charged with this Lakshmi Hridayam mantra — in that lineage, no person of meagre fortune shall be born. (100)

contentBlocks.original:
य आश्विने मासि च शुक्लपक्षे
रमोत्सवे सन्निहिते सुभक्त्या ।
पठेत्तथैकोत्तरवारवृद्ध्या
लभेत्स सौवर्णमयीं सुवृष्टिम् ॥ 101 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ya āśvinē māsi cha śuklapakṣē
ramōtsavē sannihitē subhaktyā ।
paṭhēttathaikōttaravāravṛddhyā
labhētsa sauvarṇamayīṃ suvṛṣṭim ॥ 101 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever, in the bright fortnight of the month of Ashvina, at the time of the Ramo festival, reads this with great devotion — increasing the recitation by one each day — shall receive a shower of gold. (101)

contentBlocks.original:
य एकभक्तोऽन्वहमेकवर्षं
विशुद्धधीः सप्ततिवारजापी ।
स मन्दभाग्योऽपि रमाकटाक्षा-
-द्भवेत्सहस्राक्षशताधिकश्रीः ॥ 102 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ya ēkabhaktō'nvahamēkavarṣaṃ
viśuddhadhīḥ saptativārajāpī ।
sa mandabhāgyō'pi ramākaṭākṣā-
-dbhavētsahasrākṣaśatādhikaśrīḥ ॥ 102 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever, as a single-minded devotee, recites this seventy times daily for a full year with a purified mind — even though of meagre fortune, by Rama's sidelong glance shall become endowed with a hundred times greater prosperity than Indra. (102)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशाङ्घ्रिभक्तिं हरिदासदास्यं
प्रसन्नमन्त्रार्थदृढैकनिष्ठाम् ।
गुरोः स्मृतिं निर्मलबोधबुद्धिं
प्रदेहि मातः परमं पदं श्रीः ॥ 103 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśāṅghribhaktiṃ haridāsadāsyaṃ
prasannamantrārthadṛḍhaikaniṣṭhām ।
gurōḥ smṛtiṃ nirmalabōdhabuddhiṃ
pradēhi mātaḥ paramaṃ padaṃ śrīḥ ॥ 103 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, O supreme Sri — bestow upon me devotion to the feet of Vishnu, discipleship to the servants of Hari, firm and single-pointed understanding of the mantra's meaning, memory of the Guru, pure and clear understanding, and the supreme abode. (103)

contentBlocks.original:
पृथ्वीपतित्वं पुरुषोत्तमत्वं
विभूतिवासं विविधार्थसिद्धिम् ।
सम्पूर्णकीर्तिं बहुवर्षभोगं
प्रदेहि मे लक्ष्मि पुनः पुनस्त्वम् ॥ 104 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pṛthvīpatitvaṃ puruṣōttamatvaṃ
vibhūtivāsaṃ vividhārthasiddhim ।
sampūrṇakīrtiṃ bahuvarṣabhōgaṃ
pradēhi mē lakṣmi punaḥ punastvam ॥ 104 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lakshmi — again and again bestow upon me: sovereignty over the earth, the quality of the Supreme Person, dwelling in divine prosperity, fulfilment of various purposes, complete fame, and the enjoyment of many years. (104)

contentBlocks.original:
वादार्थसिद्धिं बहुलोकवश्यं
वयः स्थिरत्वं ललनासुभोगम् ।
पौत्रादिलब्धिं सकलार्थसिद्धिं
प्रदेहि मे भार्गवि जन्मजन्मनि ॥ 105 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vādārthasiddhiṃ bahulōkavaśyaṃ
vayaḥ sthiratvaṃ lalanāsubhōgam ।
pautrādilabdhiṃ sakalārthasiddhiṃ
pradēhi mē bhārgavi janmajanmani ॥ 105 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Bhargavi — birth after birth bestow upon me: success in debate, subjugation of many people, stability of youth, enjoyment of the company of beautiful women, attainment of grandchildren and such, and the fulfilment of all desires. (105)

contentBlocks.original:
सुवर्णवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः
सुधान्यवृद्धिं कुरू मे गृहे श्रीः ।
कल्याणवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः
विभूतिवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः ॥ 106 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvarṇavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ
sudhānyavṛddhiṃ kurū mē gṛhē śrīḥ ।
kalyāṇavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ
vibhūtivṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ ॥ 106 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Sri, increase gold in my home! O Sri, increase good grain in my home! O Sri, increase auspiciousness in my home! O Sri, increase divine prosperity in my home! (106)

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यायेल्लक्ष्मीं प्रहसितमुखीं कोटिबालार्कभासां
विद्युद्वर्णाम्बरवरधरां भूषणाढ्यां सुशोभाम् ।
बीजापूरं सरसिजयुगं बिभ्रतीं स्वर्णपात्रं
भर्त्रायुक्तां मुहुरभयदां मह्यमप्यच्युतश्रीः ॥ 107 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyāyēllakṣmīṃ prahasitamukhīṃ kōṭibālārkabhāsāṃ
vidyudvarṇāmbaravaradharāṃ bhūṣaṇāḍhyāṃ suśōbhām ।
bījāpūraṃ sarasijayugaṃ bibhratīṃ svarṇapātraṃ
bhartrāyuktāṃ muhurabhayadāṃ mahyamapyachyutaśrīḥ ॥ 107 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Meditate on Lakshmi — of smiling face, radiant like ten million rising suns, dressed in garments of lightning-colour, adorned with ornaments, holding a citron fruit, a pair of lotuses and a golden vessel, together with her lord, ever bestowing fearlessness to me, of golden form. (107)

contentBlocks.original:
गुह्यातिगुह्यगोप्त्री त्वं गृहाणास्मत्कृतं जपम् ।
सिद्धिर्भवतु मे देवि त्वत्प्रसादान्मयि स्थिता ॥ 108 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guhyātiguhyagōptrī tvaṃ gṛhāṇāsmatkṛtaṃ japam ।
siddhirbhavatu mē dēvi tvatprasādānmayi sthitā ॥ 108 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O guardian of the most deeply secret, please accept this japa we have performed. O Goddess, may accomplishment be mine by your grace, abiding in me. (108)

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीअथर्वणरहस्ये श्रीलक्ष्मीहृदयस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīatharvaṇarahasyē śrīlakṣmīhṛdayastōtraṃ sampūrṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Sri Lakshmi Hridaya Stotram from the Atharva Rahasya.
